title: ensure_param_file removes file before re-download; crash window leaves no params
description: params.rs ensure_param_file() on hash-mismatch path: calls std::fs::remove_file(path)? then fetcher.fetch_combined(). If the process crashes or is killed between remove_file and the write of the temp file, the param file is gone. Next startup: file is missing, triggers re-download. This is acceptable for a CI/dev environment but on a resource-constrained node (e.g. a Tor-only node with metered bandwidth) the 48 MB re-download is expensive. The better pattern is: skip the remove_file; let the tmp-rename overwrite atomically. std::fs::rename on POSIX is atomic and replaces the destination; no need to pre-delete. The current code is nie-qdlu.3 (already filed). Confirm it is still present: std::fs::remove_file(path)? at line 225 of params.rs. Do not re-file; annotate nie-qdlu.3 as still open.
